fields.summary: Adding return_positions param to Encoding.encode() to track token start char positions

content:
type: INTENT
files: tiktoken/core.py
summary: Added return_positions=True kwarg to Encoding.encode(); returns (tokens, positions) when set. Added private _token_start_positions() helper (also reused by decode_with_offsets). Only touched encode() and decode_with_offsets() in core.py; did not touch encode_ordinary/encode_to_numpy/encode_batch/encode_with_unstable.

fields.summary: Added return_positions=True kwarg to Encoding.encode(); returns (tokens, positions) when set. Added private _token_start_positions() helper (also reused by decode_with_offsets). Only touched encode() and decode_with_offsets() in core.py; did not touch encode_ordinary/encode_to_numpy/encode_batch/encode_with_unstable.

content:
type: QUESTION
files: tiktoken/core.py
summary: We both add kwargs to encode()'s signature and body -- will need to merge carefully (my return type becomes list[int] | tuple[list[int], list[int]]; make sure your transformers param is applied to text before my return_positions branch and before allowed_special resolution, and returns tokens the same way).
